---
title: "LRP73: Is the letter-sound (L) -> word-reading (W) effect age-dependent?"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP73 is the third interaction model. It asks whether the letter-sound → word
reading effect (LRP58) **depends on age** — a developmental-readiness question:
does phonics convert to reading differently as children get older? Age was the
strongest *gain-side* signal in the SHAP screen; this tests it on the better-
powered reading level/mechanism.

The linear predictor adds an age main effect and an age interaction to the LRP58
mechanism:

$$
\eta = \dots + f^{L}(\operatorname{logit} L) + \gamma_{\text{mod}}\,z(\text{age})
       + \gamma_{\text{int}}\,z(\operatorname{logit} L)\,z(\text{age})
$$

$\gamma_{\text{int}} > 0$ would mean phonics converts to reading *more* strongly at
older ages. $W$ is not floored, so the nonparametric $f^{L}$ HSGP is retained
(unlike LRP72's linear mechanism).

::: {.callout-important title="Sharp prior expectation: age is the most confound-prone moderator"}

The two earlier interaction models found apparent interactions that were really
**between-child ability confounds**, collapsing once subject random effects +
adjustment were in (LRP71 phonics×vocab → null; LRP72 blending×letter-sound →
largely independent). **Age is even more a between-child variable** — most age
variation is across children, not within a child's four waves. So the honest
expectation is that any age moderation may also be a between-child artefact. The
within-child check below is the decisive diagnostic; this report is written to
test that honestly, not to find a positive.

:::

## Adjustment set

LRP58's $\{G, A, E, R, W_{\text{pre}}\}$.

::: {.callout-note title="LRP73 conditions on age linearly — LRP58 (by default) did not"}

In the mechanism factory, age is only entered when `use_age_gp=True` (off by
default), so LRP58 as-fit did **not** condition on age despite listing it. Here the
moderator main effect $\gamma_{\text{mod}}\,z(\text{age})$ **realises the age
adjustment linearly** — a strict improvement on LRP58's default for this question.
The moderator is therefore both the age adjustment and the thing whose interaction
with $L$ we test. $E$ and $R$ enter as confounders; $W_{\text{pre}}$ is the
autoregressive baseline; $W$ (the outcome) is of course not conditioned on.

:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

`gamma_mod` (age main effect) and `gamma_int` (L × age) share the `gamma_cross`
prior $\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$.

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Coefficients

```{python}
# | echo: false
interaction = pd.read_csv(_here / "interaction_summary.csv")
interaction.T
```

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

`gamma_int` is the age moderation. In reporting it is
$\gamma_{\text{int}} = -0.064$ (95% CI $[-0.134, +0.007]$, $P(>0) \approx 0.04$) —
**negative-leaning but not credible** (the interval includes zero). The age main
effect $\gamma_{\text{mod}} = -0.086$ ($[-0.189, +0.016]$) is likewise not
credible. Read together with the within-child check below — which is flat — there
is **no evidence that phonics converts to reading differently by age** in this
sample. (Sampling clean: R-hat 1.00, ESS ~22k, 20/36000 divergences.)

## Mechanism curve

The L → W curve $f^{L}$ at the mean age.

![Mechanism curve](mechanism_curve.png){.lightbox}

## Within-child confound check

The decisive diagnostic (`scripts/within_child_interaction_check.py`,
`output/interaction_screen/within_child_lxage_w.csv`). An OLS of logit(W) on
$z(\operatorname{logit} L)$, $z(\text{age})$ and their product, fit three ways: a
naive pooled fit (overstates the interaction via the ~3 rows/child), a
cluster-robust pooled fit, and a **within-child fixed-effects** fit (subject
dummies absorb all between-child variation, so the interaction coefficient is the
*within-child* effect).

For $L \times \text{age} \to W$ the interaction is **weak and not significant even
pooled** (naive $t \approx -1.2$) and stays small within-child ($t \approx -0.8$)
— there is no between-child signal to collapse, and none within-child either. The
companion checks reproduce the same pattern for the other two models
($L \times E$: null both ways; $L \times B$: a weak pooled positive that flips
negative within-child). The apparent screen-level age moderation does not survive
proper modelling.

## PSIS-LOO vs the no-interaction baseline

LRP73base (`docs/models/lrp73base`) is identical but with `include_interaction=
False` (age main effect, no $L \times \text{age}$). The comparison isolates the
interaction's predictive value
(`output/statistical_models/comparison/age_moderation_loo_compare.csv`):

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

The interaction model ranks first, but the ELPD advantage over the
no-interaction baseline is **modest and within noise** (elpd_diff ≈ 2, dse ≈ 1.5)
— adding the L × age term does not decisively improve predictive fit, consistent
with the non-credible `gamma_int`.

## Limitations

- **Age range + wait-list structure.** The sample's age span and the wait-list
  phase design both constrain what an "age effect" can mean here; within-child age
  variation is limited to the four assessment waves.
- This is a developmental-readiness question on observational age variation, not a
  manipulation of age.

## Interaction-phase context

LRP73 is the third of three interaction models. Taken together — phonics×vocab
(LRP71, null), blending×letter-sound (LRP72, no super-additivity), and
letter-sound×age (LRP73) — the pattern is that reading's contributing skills
combine **largely additively** in this sample, and the raw screen-level
interactions are between-child ability/age confounds rather than within-child
synergies. See the dated note for the synthesis.

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
